steps.title: Identify the triangle's sides
steps.content:
The small square marks the right angle. From the figure:
  • Hypotenuse (opposite the right angle) = 21
  • One leg = 6
  • Unknown leg = a

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
For a right triangle: $a^2+b^2=c^2$
Here $a^2+6^2=21^2$
Isolate $a^2$: $a^2=21^2-6^2$
Take the square root: $a=\sqrt{21^2-6^2}$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• "$\sqrt{{21}^{2}-{6}^{2}}$": exactly what the Pythagorean theorem gives. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
  • "${21}^{2}-{6}^{2}$": this is $a^2$, not $a$; missing the square root. Eliminated.
  • "$\sqrt{21-6}$": subtracts the sides before rooting; no Pythagorean basis. Eliminated.
  • "$21-6$": simple subtraction of sides, not valid for finding a leg. Eliminated.
